title: caltha palustris var. flabellifolia
intro: Caltha palustris var. flabellifolia is a variety of marsh marigold, a perennial plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from the alkaloids and flavonoids present in the plant. The plant is said to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting digestive health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Caltha palustris var. flabellifolia can be prepared in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
